rag_rules: Keep type-scope contract annexes in the component type owner, and keep implementation-only bindings in the component implementation owner.
When inserting into an implementation owner, place the annex after any structural declarations it depends on and preserve the surrounding AADL architecture.
generic_rules: Fuse new content into the intended owner without changing unrelated features, subcomponents, connections, or property associations.
Match annex scope to symbol visibility: type-level blocks should use only the owner's visible interface, while implementation-level blocks may align with implementation structure.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Insert AGREE content as a complete `annex agree {** ... **};` block inside the selected owner body.
Keep each annex fully enclosed within one owner and preserve normal AADL ordering and terminators around the insertion.
anti_patterns: Do not move, rename, or rewrite existing architecture elements just to accommodate an annex block.
Do not reference subcomponent paths from a type-scope annex unless those values are exposed through the owner's interface.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables
